{{Infobox location
| name = Valinhall
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Valinhall Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Valinhall, also known as Valinhall [[House]], is a unique [[Territory]] that functions as its own isolated world. It originated approximately sixty years ago from a twisted experiment by [[Damasca]], though legends suggest it may be a surviving shard of a much larger, broken world that once possessed suns and cities. The [[House]] is characterized by its unpredictable nature; it can rearrange its layout, add new rooms, and even attack or test those within it. Its internal logic is unconventional, featuring spaces like a garden that consists of open plains under a sky despite being surrounded by indoor rooms. While it is considered an inefficient way to travel because gates opened from [[the House]] typically return a person to their original entry point, it serves as a vital source of food and water through its gardens. The [[House]] is governed by the principle of force. [[The Territory]] was claimed and named by a man known as [[Valin]], also called the Wanderer. After arriving in the entry hall via a flash of light and a roar, [[Valin]] mastered much of [[the House]], including deep reaches that remain too strange for others to explore. He used his knowledge and collected books to create a [[gate]] between his world and others. [[Valin]] is considered the [[Founder]] and the [[Incarnation]] of Valinhall, a role that links him deeply to the [[Territory]]. [[Valinhall Travelers]] are distinct from those of other [[Territories]]. Rather than summoning external weapons, beasts, or elemental magic to fight, they summon power into themselves to win battles. This makes them exceptionally well-equipped for combat against other [[Territories]]. However, the number of [[Valinhall Travelers]] is dwindling following a conflict between [[Valin]] and [[Damasca]]. The relationship between a [[Traveler]] and [[the House]] can be intense; if a [[Traveler]] becomes too indebted to the [[Territory]], they face the choice of either dying or becoming an [[Incarnation]].}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Once a complete world featuring cities, trees, and a sun, Valinhall was broken into a shard that drifted into the nothingness beyond all worlds. Now existing as a [[House]] and [[Territory]] located behind an entry hall, it contains diverse landscapes such as open plains, a garden of sky, a [[library]], a forge, and a long blue-lit basement. This hostile environment tests and attacks its inhabitants to teach them, operating under a flow of time that moves slower than the outside world. While [[Simon]] settled into [[life]] there and drew [[essence]], gifts, and [[Traveler]]'s powers from the realm, he also faced fears for his [[life]] when [[Kai]] threatened his removal. Although [[Simon]] can reach Valinhall through his [[sword]], he cannot escape it by [[Traveling]] because the Gate returns a person to their starting point.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">25</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]] is a house that is also a [[Territory]] containing an entire world within its walls.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c6">''House of Blades'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]] is a house that tests and attacks its inhabitants to teach them.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Time flows slower in [[Valinhall]] than in the outside world.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] settled into [[life]] in [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7">''House of Blades'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Kai]] threatens to remove [[Simon]] from [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c8">''House of Blades'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] feared for his [[life]] in the [[Valinhall]] [[House]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Everything in [[Valinhall]] is hostile.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]] was once a world with cities, trees, and a sun before it was broken into a shard.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9">''House of Blades'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]] is a [[House]] that drifted into the nothing beyond all worlds.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]] is a [[Territory]] that differs from others in how [[Travelers]] use power.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]] contains a long blue-lit basement.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c10">''House of Blades'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]]'s layout includes a garden of sky and open plains, a [[library]], and a forge.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]] has a garden.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c11">''House of Blades'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]] is an organization or house [[Kai]] intends to give himself to.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]] is a source of [[essence]] for [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] has instincts born in [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c12">''House of Blades'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] possesses [[Traveler]]'s gifts from [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]] is located behind the entry hall.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c13">''House of Blades'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] draws power from [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c13" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] cannot escape by [[Traveling]] through [[Valinhall]] because the Gate opens wherever you had last made it.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c14">''House of Blades'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]] is a place [[Simon]] can reach through his [[sword]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c15">''House of Blades'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Caius]] was shoved into [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] reaches out to [[Valinhall]] to call upon [[steel]] strength and [[Nye]] speed.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c18">''House of Blades'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] possesses the strength and speed of [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c19">''House of Blades'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Traveling]] through [[Valinhall]] returns you to where you started.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21">''House of Blades'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Created sixty years ago as an experiment by [[Damasca]], Valinhall is a [[House]] and [[Territory]] that serves as a source of power for [[Indirial]] and certain individuals capable of wielding [[Mithra]]. This location functions as a repository where [[the Nye]] repair and store weapons, though it is reputed to be useless for rapid travel. While its [[Travelers]] excel in open combat, they lack versatility, and the territory is known to be effective at destroying foreign [[Travelers]]. Within this realm, which contains a healing pool and two women including [[Angeline]], [[Simon]] traveled to seek healing and has partially conquered the area. Although a Gate opened from Valinhall will return a traveler to its original opening point in the real world, such as the stone temple in the [[Badari Desert]], the [[Incarnation]] seeks to reshape the entire world into a harsh, combat-filled environment modeled after this place.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">30</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is a source of power that [[Indirial]] can call upon.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is a location where weapons are stored and repaired by [[the Nye]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] houses two women.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall Travelers]] excel in open combat but lack versatility.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] provides power that allows certain individuals to wield [[Mithra]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is a [[Territory]] that has a reputation for being useless for traveling quickly.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A Gate opened from [[Valinhall]] will return to the same spot in the real world where it was opened.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The stone temple at the center of the [[Badari Desert]] is a location one can return to from [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is a place the Wanderer would never choose to enter.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is a [[Territory]] that has existed for sixty years and was created as an experiment by [[Damasca]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] traveled to [[Valinhall]] for healing.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] was not equipped to support bears.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] is a [[Traveler]] of [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] has conquered [[Valinhall]] to a point.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] thought he heard dolls laughing from [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is a [[House]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c7">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] contains a healing pool.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Angeline]] is part of [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]]'s powers only last for a certain amount of time.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is a location associated with certain [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The world is shaping itself around the Wanderer to become more like [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Incarnation]] seeks to make the world like [[Valinhall]]: harsh and unforgiving, full of combat.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is effective at destroying foreign [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[frozen horn]] was attuned to [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is a name mentioned by everyone.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|There is a time difference in [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is a location.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is associated with [[Incarnations]] and [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is a location with a Gate.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valinhall]] is a place [[Simon]] might need to return to.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Valinhall is a magnificent new [[Territory]] designed to challenge those with immense power and serves as a place meant for killing other [[Travelers]]. This location features an entry room with a wooden rack, a courtyard of gray stone, and a room dedicated to honoring the dead. While it offers internal powers that can strengthen the body, provide new weapons, and allow for the summoning of items or blades, users must pay their debts to enjoy these abilities. Despite being a place where constant vigilance is the highest virtue, [[the House]] has faced an invasion and attacks from the [[King of Ragnarus]], leaving it in a state of falling apart. Ultimately, [[Simon]] serves as the leader of this [[Territory]], which maintains a two-way bond with those connected to it.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">37</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] contains a room meant for honoring the memory of those who died.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c1">''City of Light'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is a new [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is a [[Territory]] that exists to kill other [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3">''City of Light'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] contains a courtyard with gray stone columns and tiles.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4">''City of Light'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Time passes in [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6">''City of Light'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Kai]] is unable to leave [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is a place where constant vigilance is the highest virtue.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is a place one can reach out to for power.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c8">''City of Light'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Olissa]] suspects [[Simon's mask]] can draw more power from [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] contains an entry room with a wooden rack.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] has become more used to lethal threats since moving to [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is a location from which [[Simon]] could have summoned items.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is associated with powers that [[Simon]] doubts can deal with levitation.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is associated with new weapons.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12">''City of Light'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lycus]] and [[Andra]] have lived in [[Valinhall]] for several months.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13">''City of Light'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Kai]] lives in [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is a location where an invasion occurred.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] walks into [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15">''City of Light'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is falling apart.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16">''City of Light'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is a [[Territory]] that is good for fighting [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] can strengthen the body and fortify against pain.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]]'s powers are internal.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is meant to challenge those with even more power.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20">''City of Light'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is a way of reckoning time.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] believed the only colors of [[Valinhall]] were black and silver.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is described as magnificent.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The furniture in [[Valinhall]] can rise to defend it from outsiders.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Queen]] [[Cynara]] recommends [[Valinhall]] to [[Leah]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[King]] tried to destroy [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]] is a [[House]] that the [[King of Ragnarus]] has attacked.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Rhalia]] is not familiar with [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] does not have the chains of [[Valinhall]] on him.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] can summon a blade from [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] is the leader of [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c28">''City of Light'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A bond to the [[Valinhall]] [[Territory]] works both ways.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c28" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|To enjoy [[Valinhall]]'s powers, one must pay their debts.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c28" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"Legends say that, in a time before, we were more than we are. We had cities, trees, a sun. Like your own world. But that world was broken, this House one of the shards."<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"The shard drifted into the nothing beyond all worlds, and as it drifted, faded."<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“I’m not familiar with Valinhall, but even I can sense it.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate locations]]
[[Category:Structures]]
